calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-08-2025 Vacated

PLAINTIFF DEANA HICKS' MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS, (TENTATIVE RULING PART 2 OF 2)
Off Calendar Apr-16-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Steven L. Marchbanks In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Apr-16-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF DEANA HICKS' Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Deana Hicks moves for an order awarding her attorneys' fees and costs against defendant FCA US LLC. Hicks seeks $24,075 in fees, a lodestar multiplier of 1.5, and $650.30 in costs and expenses. FCA agrees that Hicks, as the prevailing party in a Song-Beverly Act lawsuit, is entitled to fees and costs but takes issue with the amount. The court awards $14,005.30 for the reasons stated herein.

"A prevailing buyer has the burden of 'showing that the fees incurred were "allowable," were "reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation," and were "reasonable in amount." ' " (Nightingale v. Hyundai Motor America (1994) 31 Cal.App.4th 99, 104 [quoting Levy v. Toyota Motor Sales, U.S.A., Inc. (1992) 4 Cal.App.4th 807, 816.) The court uses the lodestar method, determining a reasonable number of hours and a reasonable rate. The bills submitted by Hicks's counsel are entitled to a presumption of reasonableness, but upon further review the court concludes that the hours billed are excessive in light of the template nature of Song-Beverly litigation (as evidenced by identical complaints in other cases (Ex. B to Gregg Dec.)). Moreover, Hicks's time records group multiple items in a way that makes it difficult for the court to assess the reasonability of the hours billed (such as when counsel groups review of something with discussion with the client). Other entries are excessive on their face, such as nine hours billed to compiling invoices and completing timesheets or six hours to prepare a reply on the fee motion.

With respect to attorney time, the court reduces the 4/16/24 billing entry to one hour, reduces the 4/26/24 billing entry (drafting complaint) to one hour, strikes the 2.5 hour billing for creating discovery plan, strikes the one hour bill for finalizing discovery responses, and awards only two hours for the reply to the fee motion. With those adjustments, the court finds that 18.5 hours of attorney time is reasonable. For paralegal time, the court reduces the complaint-preparation time allowed to one hour and allows one hour for compilation of bills costs, and time sheets. The court finds that the hourly rates sought by Hicks's counsel for his time and his paralegal's time are reasonable. The court awards $12,025 for attorney time and $1330 for paralegal time. The court awards the costs and expenses requested by Hicks, $650.30. The court declines to award a lodestar multiplier in this straightforward action. The total amount awarded in fees and costs is $14,005.30.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Steven L. Marchbanks In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Feb-19-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF DEANA HICKS' Motion For Attorney's Fees And Costs.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The matter is ordered off calendar for failure to comply with SFLR 2.7(B) (requiring courtesy copies) and California Rule of Court 3.1300(a) (requiring service).

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
